rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for interface-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: component types for interface behavior, implementations for subcomponent bindings.
Ensure all identifiers in AGREE annexes are declared or visible in the AADL model to prevent undefined reference errors.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and should use valid AADL scope for declarations.
Component type contracts should define assumptions and guarantees for ports, while implementation contracts handle subcomponent bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes or vice versa.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and annex placement rules
